fields.name: errorMessage
fields.example: Amount should be greater than zero

processors.title: automation
processors.description:
Risk assessment engine for CLO portfolio loans.

Step 1: Map credit rating to probability of default (PD): AAA→0.01%, AA→0.02%, A→0.05%, BBB→0.20%, BB→1.00%, B→3.00%, CCC→10.00%.

Step 2: Calculate risk weight using Basel III standardized approach: AAA→0.20, AA→0.25, A→0.35, BBB→0.50, BB→0.75, B→1.00, CCC→1.50. Adjust for maturity: if maturity > 5 years, multiply risk weight by 1.15.

Step 3: Calculate capitalRequirement = loanAmount × adjustedRiskWeight × 0.08.

Step 4: Calculate expectedLoss = loanAmount × probabilityOfDefault × 0.45 (LGD assumption 45%).

Step 5: Derive riskBand from adjusted risk weight: ≤ 0.30 → "Investment Grade - Low", ≤ 0.55 → "Investment Grade - Medium", ≤ 1.00 → "Speculative - High", > 1.00 → "Speculative - Critical".

Step 6: Run Monte Carlo simulation (1000 iterations) for loan default probability:

For each iteration, generate a random number between 0 and 1
If random < probabilityOfDefault, the loan defaults in this scenario
If defaulted, apply recovery rate: loss = loanAmount × (1 - recoveryRate). Recovery rate by rating: AAA-A→0.70, BBB→0.55, BB→0.40, B→0.30, CCC→0.20
Track: number of defaults, total simulated losses
Step 7: Compute simulation results:

simulatedDefaultRate = defaults / 1000 (should approximate the PD)
expectedPortfolioLoss = average loss across all iterations
worstCaseLoss = 95th percentile loss (VaR at 95% confidence)
tailRiskLoss = average of worst 5% scenarios (CVaR / Expected Shortfall)
Step 8: Build riskNarrative: "{creditRating} loan (${loanAmount}): {riskBand}. Simulated default rate: {simulatedDefaultRate}%. Expected loss: ${expectedPortfolioLoss}. VaR(95%): ${worstCaseLoss}. Tail risk: ${tailRiskLoss}

Step 6: Run Monte Carlo simulation (1000 iterations) for loan default probability:

If random < probabilityOfDefault, the loan defaults in this scenario

readmodels.title: Portfolio Summary
readmodels.description: Aggregates per portfolio. Each LoanRiskAssessed increments totalLoans by 1, adds loanAmount to totalExposure, adds capitalRequirement to totalCapitalRequirement, adds expectedLoss to totalExpectedLoss. averageRiskWeight = weighted average of riskWeight by loanAmount. averageProbabilityOfDefault = weighted average of probabilityOfDefault by loanAmount. averageRating derived from averageRiskWeight: ≤0.25→AA, ≤0.35→A, ≤0.50→BBB, ≤0.75→BB, >0.75→B. riskBand: averageRiskWeight ≤0.55→"Investment Grade", >"Speculative". Track worstRating: the lowest credit rating in the portfolio (highest risk weight). Compare each incoming riskWeight — if higher than stored worst, update worstRating.

screens.title: Portfolio Loan Details
screens.description:
ou are filling in a screen for the lqc-fe-ai-dev pipeline. Generate the React component file src/features/Portfolio/PortfolioLoanDetails/PortfolioLoanDetails.tsx. The same component renders in two targets without code changes:

Browser SPA — mounted at /portfolio/portfolio-loan-details inside AppShell.tsx (which provides nav, auth, theming).
MCP widget — mounted inside a Claude.ai iframe via mcp-entry.generated.tsx with AppShell.mcp.tsx (no chrome). The build pipeline swaps AppShell and endpoint.generated.ts automatically; the screen file is target-agnostic.
Inputs you may read (zero-scan rule — nothing else):

TODO_CONTEXT.generated.md in this folder
.claude/skills/fe-dev/design-system-catalog.yaml
.claude/skills/fe-dev/widget-catalog.yaml
.claude/skills/fe-dev/slice-hints.yaml
Sibling generated files in this folder: types.generated.ts, fields.generated.tsx (detailRows), endpoint.generated.ts (usePortfolioLoanDetails() hook), route.generated.ts, i18n.generated.ts
Component contract:

Default export PortfolioLoanDetailsScreen — a function component, no props.
Call the generated hook to fetch the readmodel; render Loading / ErrorState / EmptyState from @lqc/ui-kit for non-success states.
On success, render the page header (title + subtitle + Refresh/Edit actions) followed by sectioned DataRows cards composed via #lib/widgets (SectionedDetailRows) — no hand-rolled atom loops.
Sections (use SectionedDetailRows):

Identity — portfolioid · loanid · borrowername · acquisitiondate · maturitydate
Financial terms — loanamount · interestrate · capitalrequirement · expectedloss
Risk profile — creditrating (pill) · riskband (pill) · probabilityofdefault (with inline progress) · simulateddefaultrate
Stress scenarios — expectedportfolioloss · tailriskloss · worstcaseloss (tint last row with error variant)
Risk narrative — full-width card with the risknarrative field as a paragraph block
Layout:

Sections 1–4 in a 2-column grid at ≥900px viewport, collapse to 1-column below 900px (MCP widgets render at ~600–800px). Use @lqc/ui-kit's Grid or Tailwind grid-cols-1 md:grid-cols-2 — no custom breakpoints.
Section 5 spans full width.
Formatting: use formatCurrency / formatPercent / formatDate from #lib/widgets. Don't hand-roll formatters. For credit rating + risk band pills use the kit's Badge / Tag family per the design-system catalog.

i18n: all visible strings (page title, section headers, button labels, error/empty copy) go through t(...) using the generated PortfolioLoanDetailsI18nKeys. No hardcoded English.
